key_insight: The paper introduces the Nanotechnology Molecular Optimization (NMO) Benchmark, showing that physics-based molecular design tasks can better test and improve generative models beyond drug-discovery proxies.
clinical_relevance: Indirect: the benchmark is not a cancer drug candidate study, but methods that generalize to harder, more realistic molecular design problems could eventually support therapeutic discovery.
method: Replaces proxy property oracles with quantum simulations
Uses strict protocols with hard structural constraints and rugged fitness landscapes
Introduces a new baseline with structural-constraint modeling and domain-agnostic pretraining
evidence_signal: Across the NMO tasks, more advanced molecular optimization methods underperformed simpler approaches, while the new baseline improved physical-property results and uncovered previously unknown structural motifs.
watch_item: The benchmark is domain-specific to nanotechnology, so gains may not transfer directly to clinical drug-discovery settings without further validation.
paper_title: Beyond Drug Discovery: The Nanotechnology Molecular Optimization (NMO) Benchmark
